data.title: Accenture in charts: FQ1 reports Y/Y revenue growth across all Industry groups
Sangamo spikes on Astellas licensing deal for gene therapy tech
data.description:
• U.S. biotech Sangamo Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SGMO) traded ~11% higher in the premarket on Thursday after the company announced a licensing agreement with Japan-based Astellas Pharma (OTCPK:ALPMF) for its gene therapy technology.
  • The agreement allows Astellas (OTCPK:ALPMY) to receive a global exclusive license for Sangamo's (NASDAQ:SGMO) neurotropic adeno-associated virus (AAV) capsid, STAC-BBB, for the development of genomic medicines for up to five targets against neurological diseases.
  • Per the deal terms, Sangamo (SGMO) will receive a $20M upfront license fee from Astellas (OTCPK:ALPMF), in addition to licensed target fees and milestone payments worth up to $1.3B. The sales-based royalties linked to the deal can reach up to mid-to-high single digits.
  • "We are delighted to license STAC-BBB to Astellas to advance potential treatments for neurological diseases with significant unmet medical needs," remarked Sangamo (SGMO) CEO Sandy Macrae.

data.title: Darden Restaurants declares $1.40 dividend
data.description:
• Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) declared $1.40/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.5%
  • Payable Feb. 3; for shareholders of record Jan. 10; ex-div Jan. 10.
  • See DRI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Starwood Property Trust to boost investing pace in 2025, following December capital transactions
data.description:
With capital markets transactions completed in December, Starwood Property Trust (NYSE:STWD) extended its weighted average corporate debt maturity to 3.5 years from 2.2 years and now has no corporate debt maturities until July 2026, the company said Thursday.

The transactions in December 2024 lowered the company's borrowing costs and created $783M of new capital to deploy, That will allow the company to boost its investing pace in 2025, the company said, citing the thawing of commercial real estate capital markets, which creates lending opportunities, its pipeline of Q4 and Q1 investments, and its incremental low-cost capital positions.

"With our heightened level of investment activity, we are pleased to have had the opportunity to again increase our liquidity with these transactions, while reducing our cost of funds and extending the tenor of our debt," Chairman and CEO Barry Sternlicht said.

Starwood Property Trust (NYSE:STWD) stock rose 1.2% in premarket trading.

The December transactions included:

  • The pricing of a new issue of $500M 6.500% senior unsecured sustainability notes due July 2030, swapped to SOFR + 2.25%, the lowest spread in Starwood Property Trust's (STWD) history.
  • Amending the company's $590M term loan B due November 2027 at par. The transaction was upsized by $100M to $690M and its spread reduced by 0.50% to SOFR + 2.25%.
  • Repaying December 2024 and March 2025 unsecured notes.

data.title: Altimmune set to join Nasdaq Biotechnology index
data.description:
• Altimmune (NASDAQ:ALT) has announced that it will be added to the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index, effective prior to the market open on Monday, December 23, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dollar Tree appoints Michael Creedon permanent CEO
data.description:
• Retail discount stores operator Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) announced the appointment of interim CEO Michael Creedon as permanent CEO.
  • Creedon had joined Dollar Tree in 2022 as COO.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold79 Mines to change name to West Point Gold
data.description:
• Gold79 Mines (OTCQB:AUSVF) said it will change its name to West Point Gold, and will trade under a new symbol on the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • The new stock symbol is WPG.
  • The change will be effective at market open on December 23.

data.title: CareTrust REIT doubles revolving credit facility to $1.2B
data.description:
• CareTrust REIT (NYSE:CTRE) has renewed and doubled its unsecured revolving credit facility to $1.2B.
  • The bank group consisted of joint lead arranger/administrative agent KeyBanc Capital Markets, and joint lead arrangers/syndication agents: BMO Capital Markets Corp., JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A., Bank of America, N.A., Wells Fargo Securities, LLC, and co-documentation agents: The Huntington National Bank, Raymond James Bank, M&T Bank, Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., and Royal Bank of Canada.
  • The REIT also said its investment pipeline currently stands at approximately $350M (not including larger portfolio opportunities the trust is reviewing).

data.title: Tripadvisor to buy Liberty TripAdvisor
data.description:
Tripadvisor (NASDAQ:TRIP) on Thursday announced an agreement to acquire Liberty TripAdvisor Holdings (OTCQB:LTRPA) (OTCQB:LTRPB) for an aggregate transaction value of approximately $435M.

The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2025.

Pursuant to the merger agreement, shares of Liberty TripAdvisor Series A Common Stock and Series B Common Stock issued and outstanding immediately prior to the effective time of the merger will be converted into the right to receive $0.2567 a share in cash (without interest), totaling approximately $20M in the aggregate.

Liberty TripAdvisor owns ~16.45M shares of Tripadvisor common stock and 12.79M shares of Tripadvisor Class B common stock, of ~2.42M shares of Tripadvisor common stock are pledged as collateral against Liberty TripAdvisor's variable prepaid forward contract (the "VPF").

Upon consummation of the deal, Tripadvisor plans to retire approximately 27M shares of Tripadvisor common stock currently held by Liberty TripAdvisor, net of ~2.4M shares underlying the VPF.

Tripadvisor views this transaction as effectively a repurchase of its common stock held by Liberty TripAdvisor. The implied share price for the acquisition of such shares is $16.21, which reflects a premium of ~16% based on the 10-day volume-weighted average share price as of Dec. 17, 2024.

TripAdvisor (TRIP) shares rose 8.88% premarket.

data.title: Accenture rises after Q1 beat as AI bookings continue to grow, raises revenue outlook
data.description:
Accenture's (NYSE:ACN) stock rose about 6% premarket on Thursday after fiscal first quarter results beat expectations and raised its revenue growth outlook for fiscal 2025.

However, the revenue growth outlook was slightly below estimates.

For the fiscal first quarter ended Nov. 30, revenue climbed 9% (in U.S. dollar terms) year-on-year to $ $17.7B, surpassing analysts' estimates.

"We delivered broad-based revenue growth across both consulting and managed services, and across each market and industry group, gaining market share. First quarter new bookings were $18.7 billion, including 30 quarterly client bookings of more than $100 million, and we continued to lead in helping our clients realize value with generative AI, with new bookings of $1.2 billion," said Accenture's Chair and CEO Julie Sweet.

Generative AI new bookings for the fiscal fourth quarter, ended Aug. 31, were $1B.

The Irish company's Consulting revenues in the first quarter grew 7% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year to $$9.05B, while Managed Services revenues jumped 11% (in U.S. dollars) to $$8.64B.

However, new bookings for the first quarter rose only 1% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year to $18.70B.

The company's total cash balance at Nov. 30 was $8.3B compared to $5B at Aug. 31, 2024.

Dividend: Accenture declared a $1.48 quarterly dividend, the same as the previous quarter's amount. The record date is Jan. 16, 2025 and the dividend is payable on Feb. 14, 2025.

Stock Buyback: During the first quarter, Accenture bought back or redeemed 2.5M shares, including about 2.2M shares repurchased in the open market, for a total of $$898M. Accenture's total remaining share repurchase authority at Nov. 30, was around $5.9B.

Outlook:

Second quarter fiscal 2025: Accenture expects revenues for the second quarter to be in the range of $16.2B to $16.8B (midpoint at $16.5B), below consensus estimate of $16.62B.

Fiscal year 2025: The company raises revenue growth to be in the range of 4% to 7% (midpoint at 5%) in local currency, compared to 3% to 6% previously, consensus revenue growth is 5.61%.

Accenture now expects GAAP diluted EPS for fiscal 2025 to be in the range of $12.43 to $12.79 (midpoint $12.61%) compared to $12.55 to $12.91 previously.

The company said it continues to expect to return at least $8.3B in cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

Peers, Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH) rose about 1%, while EPAM Systems (EPAM) and Infosys (INFY) were largely flat premarket on Thursday.
